The New Haven Documentary Film Festival (NHdocs) is an annual event held in New Haven, Connecticut, over a weekend in June. It features a variety of film screenings, including documentaries, talks, panels, and workshops. The festival takes place at the New Haven Public Library, located at 300 Main Street, New Haven, CT. The event is free and open to all audiences, with seating arrangements designed to accommodate diverse needs. Attendees can engage with filmmakers and special guests, attend panels, and participate in meet-and-greets. The festival has a rich history, having been running since 1992, and has grown significantly, earning a reputation for its impact on the documentary film community. Promotions include social media campaigns and partnerships with industry influencers. Attendees are encouraged to plan their visit by checking the schedule for specific dates, sessions, and ticket availability, ensuring they arrive in New Haven during the festival weekend.
